objects.name: PAP
objects.description: This defines PAP as a STIX extension

objects.name: Dindoor Deployment
objects.description: Iranian APT MuddyWater is deploying a new backdoor called 'Dindoor' and a Python-based malware 'Fakeset' targeting U.S. banks, airports, and nonprofits.

objects.content:
Sector: Finance, Aviation, Non-profit, Defense

Modus operandi:
Use of Deno runtime to execute JS/TS code, signed certificates, and data exfiltration via Rclone to Wasabi cloud storage.

objects.name: Iraqi Ministry Impersonation
objects.description: Suspected Iran-nexus group targeting Iraqi government officials with phishing emails delivering custom .NET backdoors.

objects.content:
Sector: Government

Modus operandi:
Phishing with password-protected archives, DLL side-loading (VLC, WingetUI), and AI-assisted malware development (SPLITDROP, TWINTALK).

objects.name: Jasper Sleet / Coral Sleet AI Ops
objects.description: North Korean remote IT workers leveraging generative AI to create fraudulent digital personas and accelerate malware engineering.

objects.content:
Sector: Technology, Multiple

Modus operandi:
Use of LLMs for culturally appropriate persona building, GAN-based domain generation, and 'agentic' AI for end-to-end lure development.

objects.name: CVE-2026-20127
objects.description:
Maximum-severity authentication bypass in peering mechanism allowing full administrative access. Exploited since 2023 by UAT-8616.

Affected product: Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager

Affected vendor: Cisco

CPE: cpe:/a:cisco:catalyst_sd-wan_manager

objects.name: CVE-2026-25070
objects.description:
OS command injection in the /goform/PingTestSet endpoint via destIp parameter allowing remote code execution as root.

Affected product: SKS8310-8X Network Switch

Affected vendor: XikeStor

CPE: cpe:/h:xikestor:sks8310-8x

objects.name: CVE-2017-7921
objects.description:
Legacy improper authentication vulnerability added to CISA KEV due to ongoing exploitation in 2026.

Affected product: IP Cameras

Affected vendor: Hikvision

CPE: cpe:/h:hikvision:ip_camera

objects.name: Breach: FBI
objects.description:
Breach of systems used to manage surveillance and wiretap warrants. Address by FBI after identifying 'suspicious activity'.

Sector: Law Enforcement

objects.name: Breach: Cognizant TriZetto
objects.description:
Data breach exposing sensitive health and insurance data of 3.4 million patients. Unauthorized access lasted from Nov 2024 to Oct 2025.

Sector: Healthcare IT

objects.abstract: Geopolitical analysis (EN) / Middle East Conflict & Disinformation
objects.content:
Sector: International Relations

Theme: Middle East Conflict & Disinformation

The war involving the US, Israel, and Iran (started Feb 2026) is accompanied by massive Russian disinformation campaigns. Russia is attempting to position itself as a peace guarantor while spreading false claims about Ukrainian/Western nuclear plots and sabotage.

Tags: Russia, Iran, Disinformation

objects.name: InstallFix Social Engineering Technique
objects.description:
A variant of ClickFix targeting developers by pushing malicious 'curl-to-bash' commands via fake CLI tool installation guides (e.g., Claude Code).

Analysis:
Attackers exploit the 'trust the domain' model and the common developer practice of copying terminal commands. Payloads like Amatera and Lumma steal browser credentials and crypto wallets.

Recommendations:
- Verify installation commands from official primary documentation sources only.
- Use browser protection tools to flag newly created typosquatted domains.

objects.content: The cybersecurity landscape as of March 7, 2026, is dominated by three major trends: the mass operationalization of Artificial Intelligence by both attackers and defenders, a significant increase in zero-day exploitation targeting enterprise edge infrastructure, and heightened state-sponsored activity linked to Middle Eastern geopolitical tensions. Google's GTIG report confirms a record share of zero-days (48%) targeting enterprise technologies. AI is now a central 'tradecraft' tool, with groups like Jasper Sleet (North Korea) using LLMs for fraudulent personas and malware development, while defenders like Mozilla and Elastic use AI to accelerate vulnerability discovery and exploit analysis. The breach of FBI surveillance management systems and the massive healthcare data exposure at Cognizant TriZetto highlight the critical vulnerability of sensitive governmental and medical data repositories.
